    $ uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 --cluster lkc-111111 --cluster lkc-222222

Expected output: 

[INFO] Discovering environments...
[INFO] Found 3 environments: env-abc123, env-def456, env-ghi789
[INFO] Discovering clusters...
[INFO] Found 7 clusters across 3 environments
[INFO] Extracting from env-abc123 (2 clusters)...
[INFO] Phase 1: KafkaAdmin (lkc-111111)
[INFO]   - Found 23 topics, 5 consumer groups
[INFO] Phase 1: KafkaAdmin (lkc-222222)
[INFO]   - Found 18 topics, 3 consumer groups
[INFO] Phase 2: Connect, ksqlDB, Flink (parallel)
[INFO]   - Connect: 4 connectors
[INFO]   - ksqlDB: 2 queries
[INFO]   - Flink: 1 statement
[INFO] Phase 3: SchemaRegistry, StreamCatalog (parallel)
[INFO]   - SchemaRegistry: 41 schemas
[INFO] Phase 4: Tableflow
[INFO]   - 12 topics mapped to UC tables
[INFO] Extraction complete: 142 nodes, 218 edges
[INFO] Graph saved to lineage_graph.json

docs.title: Per-Cluster Credentials
docs.text: By default, LineageBridge uses your cloud API key for all operations. If you have cluster-scoped API keys, you can configure them per cluster.

docs.title: Credential Resolution Order
docs.text:
For each cluster, LineageBridge tries:

  1. Per-cluster credential (from CLUSTER_CREDENTIALS map)
  2. Global Kafka credential (KAFKA_API_KEY / KAFKA_API_SECRET)
  3. Cloud API key (fallback)

docs.title: Option 1: Environment Variable
docs.text:
Set a JSON map in your .env file:

LINEAGE_BRIDGE_CLUSTER_CREDENTIALS={"lkc-111111":{"api_key":"AAAA","api_secret":"xxxx"},"lkc-222222":{"api_key":"BBBB","api_secret":"yyyy"}}

Format: 

{
  "lkc-111111": {
    "api_key": "AAAA...",
    "api_secret": "xxxx..."
  },
  "lkc-222222": {
    "api_key": "BBBB...",
    "api_secret": "yyyy..."
  }
}

Example .env snippet: 

LINEAGE_BRIDGE_CONFLUENT_CLOUD_API_KEY=cloud-level-key
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_CONFLUENT_CLOUD_API_SECRET=cloud-level-secret
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_CLUSTER_CREDENTIALS={"lkc-abc123":{"api_key":"cluster-key-1","api_secret":"secret-1"},"lkc-def456":{"api_key":"cluster-key-2","api_secret":"secret-2"}}

docs.title: Option 2: UI Entry
docs.text:
1. Start the UI and expand an environment in the sidebar
  2. Click the gear icon next to a cluster
  3. Enter the cluster-scoped API key and secret
  4. Click "Save"

The credentials are encrypted and stored in ~/.lineage_bridge/cache.json using Fernet symmetric encryption.

docs.title: Option 3: Global Kafka Credential
docs.text:
If all clusters use the same cluster-scoped key:

LINEAGE_BRIDGE_KAFKA_API_KEY=common-cluster-key
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_KAFKA_API_SECRET=common-cluster-secret

This applies to all clusters that don't have a per-cluster credential.

docs.title: Use Case 1: Separate Dev/Staging/Prod Environments
docs.text:
Extract from all three environments to see lineage across your SDLC:

$ uv run lineage-bridge-extract \
    --env env-dev \
    --env env-staging \
    --env env-prod

The graph will show nodes from all three environments, with edges connecting topics that share data across environments.

Node IDs include the environment: 

confluent:kafka_topic:env-dev:orders
confluent:kafka_topic:env-staging:orders
confluent:kafka_topic:env-prod:orders

Each is a separate node. Cross-environment connectors or Flink jobs create edges between them.

docs.title: Use Case 2: Regional Deployments
docs.text:
You have one environment per region (US, EU, APAC):

$ uv run lineage-bridge-extract \
    --env env-us-east \
    --env env-eu-west \
    --env env-apac-southeast

The graph shows regional isolation or cross-region replication (via connectors or MirrorMaker).

docs.title: Use Case 3: Per-Team Environments
docs.text:
Extract from all team environments to understand inter-team dependencies:

$ uv run lineage-bridge-extract

Auto-discovery finds all environments, including: - env-team-payments - env-team-orders - env-team-notifications

The graph reveals which teams consume data from other teams.

docs.title: Filter by Environment
docs.text:
Only extract from specific environments:

$ uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 --env env-def456

docs.title: Filter by Cluster
docs.text:
Only extract from specific clusters within an environment:

$ uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 --cluster lkc-111111

Useful when an environment has many clusters but you only care about one.

docs.title: Combine Filters
docs.text:
Filter by both environment and cluster:

$ uv run lineage-bridge-extract \
    --env env-abc123 --cluster lkc-111111 \
    --env env-def456 --cluster lkc-222222

This extracts only the specified clusters from the specified environments.

docs.title: Parallel Extraction
docs.text:
LineageBridge extracts from clusters sequentially (Phase 1: KafkaAdmin) but processes transformation layers in parallel (Phase 2: Connect, ksqlDB, Flink).

Extraction time scales with: - Number of topics (most significant) - Number of connectors, ksqlDB queries, Flink statements - Network latency to Confluent Cloud

Typical timings: - Small environment (10 topics, 2 clusters): ~10 seconds - Medium environment (100 topics, 5 clusters): ~30 seconds - Large environment (1000 topics, 10 clusters): ~2 minutes

docs.title: Caching
docs.text:
The UI caches environment and cluster discovery results for 5 minutes. To force a refresh, restart the UI.

The CLI always performs fresh discovery.

docs.title: "No environments found"
docs.text:
Cause: Your cloud API key lacks read access to environments.

Solution: 1. Verify your API key has OrgAdmin or EnvironmentAdmin role: 

$ confluent api-key describe <key> --resource cloud

  1. Try listing environments manually: 

    $ confluent environment list

  2. If that fails, your key doesn't have the required permissions. Create a new cloud API key with broader access.

docs.title: "Cluster discovery failed for env-xyz"
docs.text:
Cause: The cloud API key can see the environment but not its clusters.

Solution: 1. Check cluster list access: 

$ confluent kafka cluster list --environment env-xyz

  1. If you see clusters, the issue is with LineageBridge. Check logs for HTTP errors.

  2. If you don't see clusters, your key lacks CloudClusterAdmin access. Grant it via the Confluent Cloud UI.

docs.title: "401 Unauthorized on cluster lkc-xyz"
docs.text:
Cause: The API key used for that cluster lacks read access to Kafka topics.

Solution: 1. Check which credential is being used. LineageBridge logs the credential resolution order.

  1. If using per-cluster credentials, verify the key has read access: 

    $ confluent kafka topic list --cluster lkc-xyz --api-key <key> --api-secret <secret>

  2. If using the cloud API key, it may not have cluster-level read access. Create a cluster-scoped API key: 

    $ confluent api-key create --resource lkc-xyz --description "LineageBridge read-only"

Then add it to CLUSTER_CREDENTIALS in your .env file.

docs.title: "Extraction is slow for large environments"
docs.text:
Cause: Processing many topics sequentially.

Solution: 1. Use cluster filtering to extract only relevant clusters: 

$ uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 --cluster lkc-primary

  1. Disable optional extractors if you don't need them:
  2. In the UI: uncheck Schema Registry, Stream Catalog, or Tableflow in the sidebar

  3. In the CLI: not yet supported (all extractors run by default)

  4. Run extractions in parallel for different environments (separate processes): 

    $ uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 > graph-abc.json &
$ uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-def456 > graph-def.json &
$ wait

Then merge the graphs manually (no built-in merge tool yet).

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Configure per-cluster credentials properly to avoid permission errors
  • Set up Docker deployment to run multi-environment extractions in containers
  • Automate extraction with CI/CD to keep lineage up to date

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: All notable changes to LineageBridge are documented here. The format follows Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: [Unreleased]
docs.text: See the latest commits for work in progress.
Modularity refactor: services layer, pluggable storage, watcher as an independent service. Plus a stack of UX + reliability fixes that fell out during validation.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Service layer (lineage_bridge/services/) — single entry point for extraction / enrichment / push (run_extraction, run_enrichment, run_push). UI, API, and watcher all call into it with the same ExtractionRequest / EnrichmentRequest / PushRequest shape. (Phase 1A, ADR-020)
  • Pluggable storage layer (lineage_bridge/storage/) with three backends:
  • memory (default) — process-local, ephemeral
  • file — JSON files + flock-guarded writes
  • sqlite — single storage.db, WAL mode, versioned-SQL migrations, durable across restarts; recommended for the watcher. Selected via LINEAGE_BRIDGE_STORAGE__BACKEND={memory,file,sqlite}. (Phases 1C + 2F, ADR-022)
  • Catalog protocol v2 — UC_TABLE / GLUE_TABLE / GOOGLE_TABLE collapsed into one CATALOG_TABLE node type with a catalog_type discriminator (UNITY_CATALOG / AWS_GLUE / GOOGLE_DATA_LINEAGE / AWS_DATAZONE). Adding a new catalog = one file in catalogs/. Push surface unified through services.run_push(PushRequest). (Phase 1B, ADR-021 — clean break, no migration; old graph JSON raises a ValidationError on load)
  • Watcher as an independent peer service — split into WatcherService (pure-logic state machine, no threading), WatcherRunner (asyncio loop + persistence), WatcherRepository (memory + sqlite backends), and api/routers/watcher.py (6 endpoints: start / stop / status / events / history / list / deregister). Two run modes: lineage-bridge-watch daemon (production, survives UI restarts) or in-process API task (development). UI now polls GET /api/v1/watcher/{id}/* via httpx and holds only the watcher_id string. (Phase 2G, ADR-023)
  • POST /api/v1/push/{provider} endpoint — the API gains feature parity with the UI's "Push to X" buttons. (Phase 1A)
  • Orchestrator phase abstraction — each phase implements Phase.execute(ctx) and is independently testable. (Phase 1D)
  • Per-demo credential cache accumulation — switching .env between UC / Glue / BQ / DataZone demos no longer wipes the previous demo's credentials. The ~/.lineage_bridge/cache.json deep-merges per-cluster / per-env credential dicts; the demo provision scripts mirror Databricks workspace + AWS region into the cache too.
  • Metrics enrichment for every node type (with --metrics): Telemetry-based for topics / connectors / Flink jobs; consumer-group lag → metrics_total_lag; tableflow inherits from upstream topic; catalog tables get metrics_active from the most recent of last_modified_time / updated_at / update_time / create_time; ksqlDB queries from state. New --metrics and --metrics-lookback-hours CLI flags.
  • DLQ topic wiring — sink connectors now expose their internal lcc-XXXXX resource ID via expand=info,status,id. The Connect extractor emits a placeholder dlq-{lcc-id} topic + PRODUCES edge, which merges with the real topic on the kafka_admin pass. DLQ topics no longer appear as orphan nodes; renderer surfaces a red "D" badge variant.
  • Sidebar legend variants — per-catalog brand rows (UC / Glue / BigQuery / DataZone) plus "DLQ topic" and "Topic with schema" badge rows when present in the graph.
  • Settings.api_url — UI uses this to find the API (default http://127.0.0.1:8000); set when API runs in a separate container.
  • AWS partition-aware Glue console deeplinks — _console_host(region) maps GovCloud (us-gov-*) and China (cn-*) regions to the right console hostname.
  • Storage conformance suite — same 24 tests run against memory + file + sqlite (72 cases), plus 12 watcher-repository tests × memory + sqlite (24 cases), plus 11 sqlite-specific tests + 2 sqlite API-integration tests.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• UI sidebar decomposed — the 1,077-LOC ui/sidebar.py split into ui/sidebar/{__init__,connection,scope,credentials,actions,filters}.py. CSS extracted from ui/app.py to ui/static/styles.css. Sample graph promoted from imperative builder to bundled ui/static/sample_graph.json. (Phase 2E)
  • Per-catalog console deep-link labels — was hardcoded "Open in BigQuery" on every CATALOG_TABLE (visible on UC + Glue tables too); now dispatches per catalog_type (Open in Unity Catalog / Open in AWS Glue / Open in BigQuery). Duplicate Glue button removed.
  • Per-catalog brand icons reach the actual graph nodes — render_graph_raw and render_graph now wire icon_for_node(node) so UC / Glue / BigQuery / DataZone tables render with their brand icon (was: every catalog table got the generic database icon).
  • DataZone live integration tests gate on iam:SimulatePrincipalPolicy — when the caller lacks datazone:CreateFormType / CreateAssetType, the tests skip cleanly with the IAM diff in the skip reason rather than failing on AccessDeniedException. The graceful-degradation path is still exercised by the manual provider.push_lineage smoke.
  • Dataplex live integration test uses deterministic GET-by-name instead of LIST entries → find by FQN to avoid the eventual-consistency flake on Dataplex Catalog list endpoints.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• Glue catalog deep-link region — build_url no longer silently falls back to us-east-1 when the node has no aws_region. build_node now stamps aws_region from the configured provider region (or Tableflow CI region) at build time, and build_url returns None if no region is available anywhere (no more wrong-region buttons).
  • Google catalog table_name parity — GoogleLineageProvider.build_node was producing different node IDs than connect.py:_build_google_tables for the same logical table (split-on-dot vs replace-on-dot). Fixed to mirror the Connect path.
  • ksqlDB extraction 401 — when Settings.ksqldb_api_key is unset, the processing phase now falls back to the cached lineage-bridge-ksqldb-{env_id} provisioned SA key. Same pattern applied to the Tableflow phase.
  • CSS escape bug — ui/static/styles.css header comment contained the literal text </style>, which the browser parsed as closing the wrapping <style> opened by st.markdown, dumping the rest of the CSS as plain text on the page.
  • Streamlit cluster_select widget warning — the multiselect was passing both key= and default=. Pre-seed st.session_state["cluster_select"] instead, drop the default arg, prune stale labels.
  • Watcher push path — the old WatcherEngine imported run_glue_push / run_lineage_push (both deleted in Phase 1B); the new service uses services.run_push via PushRequest, the same path the UI + API hit.

docs.title: Removed
docs.text:
• lineage_bridge/watcher/engine.py:WatcherEngine — replaced by services.WatcherService + services.WatcherRunner. The threading wrapper is gone (the daemon owns its asyncio loop directly). The _use_audit_log private property is gone (mode is explicit in WatcherConfig.mode). The CLI no longer calls a private _run_loop() directly.
  • run_glue_push / run_lineage_push wrappers in extractors/orchestrator.py — the catalog protocol v2 dispatches via services.run_push(PushRequest) instead. (Phase 1B)
  • UC_TABLE / GLUE_TABLE / GOOGLE_TABLE NodeType members — collapsed to CATALOG_TABLE + catalog_type. (Phase 1B; clean break per ADR-021)

docs.title: Tests
docs.text:
• 813 → 892 baseline pytest, 7 skipped (live cloud integration; opt-in via LINEAGE_BRIDGE_*_INTEGRATION=1).
  • New conformance suite covers memory + file + sqlite for graphs / tasks / events; memory + sqlite for watchers.
  • Lint clean (ruff, line length 100).
New catalog integrations, brand-icon refresh, BigQuery node enhancement, and CI/docs hardening.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• AWS DataZone provider (AWSDataZoneProvider, DataZoneAssetRegistrar): registers Kafka topics as DataZone assets with schema and posts OpenLineage events via post_lineage_event. Mirrors the Google Dataplex / Data Lineage architecture for AWS.
  • Dataplex Catalog asset registration (DataplexAssetRegistrar): each Kafka topic becomes a Dataplex entry with the same FQN as the lineage event, so the BigQuery Lineage tab shows column metadata on upstream Confluent nodes (events alone don't carry schema — Google strips facets at storage).
  • Rich lineage_bridge.upstream_chain payload in UC TBLPROPERTIES, UC bridge table (chain_json column), Glue Parameters, and Glue Description: full multi-hop chain including Flink/ksqlDB SQL, intermediate topics, source connectors, and per-topic schema fields. Capped per catalog's value-size limit.
  • Multi-hop OpenLineage push for Google: every Job-event (source connectors, Flink, ksqlDB, sinks) is pushed so the Lineage tab can walk transitively from a BQ table back to the source topics.
  • Live integration tests: tests/integration/test_gcp_dataplex_integration.py (gated by LINEAGE_BRIDGE_GCP_INTEGRATION=1) and tests/integration/test_aws_datazone_integration.py (gated by LINEAGE_BRIDGE_AWS_DATAZONE_INTEGRATION=1). Run via make test-integration-dataplex / make test-integration-datazone.
  • "Push to DataZone" button in the Streamlit publish panel, gated on LINEAGE_BRIDGE_AWS_DATAZONE_DOMAIN_ID + LINEAGE_BRIDGE_AWS_DATAZONE_PROJECT_ID.
  • Official brand icons for Kafka, Flink, Databricks (Unity Catalog), Google BigQuery, and AWS Glue graph nodes. Other node types keep their geometric icons. New _IconSpec config supports logo and tile render modes plus an optional fill_override for recolouring monochrome marks.
  • Shell-based integration test harnesses for all three demo environments: scripts/integration-test-uc.sh (7 tests), scripts/integration-test-glue.sh (8 tests), scripts/integration-test-bigquery.sh (9 tests). Each validates extraction, enrichment, catalog push, API, watcher, and Docker, with --skip-docker for CI.
  • Documentation polish: prominent docs-site link on the README, deep-links from catalog feature bullets to per-catalog guides, GCP environment variables documented, lineage-bridge-api entrypoint surfaced.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Shared OpenLineage namespace normalizer (api/openlineage/normalize.py) — used by both Google and DataZone providers, parametrised by allowlist ({bigquery} for Google, {kafka, aws} and {bigquery, aws} for DataZone).
  • Shared upstream-chain builder (catalogs/upstream_chain.py) — single source of truth for chain shape, used by all four catalogs.
  • google-auth is now a hard dependency (was previously imported lazily and silently failed if missing).
  • BigQuery connector lineage: BigQuery sink connectors now synthesise per-topic GOOGLE_TABLE nodes in clients/connect.py so the publish UI surfaces them directly without requiring Tableflow.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• Deploy Documentation workflow: 9 broken cross-reference links in docs/how-to/index.md, docs/reference/glossary.md, and docs/demos/index.md retargeted to existing pages so mkdocs build --strict passes.
  • CI lint: import ordering + formatter applied to lineage_bridge/api/routers/tasks.py (had been failing ruff check on every PR).
  • IAM trust policy corrections and Interactive API Explorer fix (use built-in Swagger UI).
  • 7 bugs discovered during integration-test validation (full list in docs/INTEGRATION_TESTS_BUG_FIXES.md).

Major Features:

  • Multi-demo infrastructure support for parallel Confluent environments
  • Google Data Lineage API provider integration
  • OpenLineage API server with FastAPI backend
  • Comprehensive MkDocs Material documentation site

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• OpenLineage API server at /api/v1/lineage with Swagger UI at /docs
  • Google Data Lineage provider for GCP integration
  • Dataset Lineage API documentation and reference
  • Interactive API explorer with built-in Swagger UI

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Refactored API Reference with tabs and practical examples

  • Refactored Catalog Integration with improved diagrams
  • Switched from custom Scalar to built-in Swagger UI

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• IAM trust policy for self-assume role in AWS Glue integration
  • OAuth secret creation error handling in Databricks setup
  • Databricks CLI auth environment variable parsing
  • Docker workflow to use correct Dockerfile path
  • Broken links in Google Data Lineage documentation

Major Features:

  • Change-detection watcher with REST polling and debounced re-extraction
  • AWS Glue catalog provider integration

  • Interactive demo infrastructure with Terraform

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Change-detection watcher CLI (lineage-bridge-watch)
  • REST polling mode for change detection (10s interval, 30s debounce)
  • Audit log Kafka consumer mode (retained for future use)
  • AWS Glue catalog provider with metadata enrichment

docs.title: Upgrading to v0.4.0
docs.text:
Breaking Changes:

  • Documentation moved from port 8000 to 8001 to avoid API server conflict
  • Docker file paths changed to infra/docker/ - update any custom compose files

New Features:

  • OpenLineage API available at http://localhost:8000/api/v1/lineage
  • Interactive API docs at http://localhost:8000/docs
  • Google Data Lineage provider enabled via LINEAGE_BRIDGE_GOOGLE_PROJECT_ID

docs.title: Upgrading to v0.3.0
docs.text:
Breaking Changes:

  • Environment variable format changed: CLUSTER_CREDENTIALS now uses JSON map instead of flat KAFKA_API_KEY
  • Docker files moved from root to infra/docker/ - update references in CI/CD
  • Scripts moved to infra/ directory

New Features:

  • Change-detection watcher: uv run lineage-bridge-watch
  • AWS Glue support: set LINEAGE_BRIDGE_AWS_REGION in .env
  • Databricks lineage push: automatically writes to UC table properties

Migration Steps:

  1. Update .env file format (see .env.example)
  2. Update Docker Compose paths: docker-compose.yml → infra/docker/docker-compose.yml
  3. Run make format to apply new code style rules

docs.title: Upgrading to v0.2.0
docs.text:
Breaking Changes:

  • app.py decomposed into multiple modules - custom imports need updates
  • Graph component switched from streamlit-agraph to custom vis.js component

New Features:

  • Catalog providers: add Databricks or Glue credentials to .env
  • Metrics API: enriches graphs with throughput data (opt-in)

Migration Steps:

  1. Install updated dependencies: uv pip install -e ".[dev]"
  2. Update any custom UI code to use new module structure
  3. Run make test to validate changes

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text: A comprehensive reference of terms used in LineageBridge, Confluent Cloud, and data catalog integrations.

docs.title: API Key
docs.text: Credential pair (key + secret) used to authenticate with Confluent Cloud APIs. LineageBridge supports both cloud-level and cluster-scoped API keys.

docs.title: Async
docs.text: Asynchronous programming pattern used throughout LineageBridge clients. All Confluent API calls use httpx with async/await for non-blocking I/O.

docs.title: Audit Log
docs.text: Kafka topic that records Confluent Cloud API activity. LineageBridge can consume this for change detection (retained for future use).

docs.title: AWS Glue
docs.text: Amazon Web Services data catalog service. LineageBridge integrates with Glue via the GlueProvider to enrich tables and push lineage metadata.

docs.title: BYOB (Bring Your Own Bucket)
docs.text: Tableflow integration mode where data is staged in a customer-managed S3 bucket before loading into a data catalog.

docs.title: Catalog Provider
docs.text: Implementation of the CatalogProvider protocol that integrates a data catalog (e.g., Databricks UC, AWS Glue, Google Data Lineage). Providers implement build_node(), enrich(), build_url(), and push_lineage().

docs.title: Client
docs.text: Module that interacts with a specific Confluent Cloud API. All clients extend ConfluentClient base class and implement retry, pagination, and error handling.

docs.title: Cluster
docs.text: Kafka cluster within a Confluent Cloud environment. LineageBridge supports extracting lineage from multiple clusters in parallel.

docs.title: CloudEvent
docs.text: Standard format for event data. LineageBridge parses audit log events using the CloudEvent specification.

docs.title: Connector
docs.text: Kafka Connect connector (source or sink) that moves data between Kafka topics and external systems. LineageBridge extracts connector metadata and creates edges to external datasets.

docs.title: Consumer Group
docs.text: Group of Kafka consumers that coordinate to consume messages from topics. LineageBridge tracks consumer group membership via the KafkaAdmin client.

docs.title: DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph)
docs.text: Graph structure where edges have direction and no cycles exist. LineageBridge renders lineage as a DAG using Sugiyama layout.

docs.title: Databricks
docs.text: Unified analytics platform. LineageBridge integrates with Databricks via Unity Catalog and the SQL Statement Execution API.

docs.title: Dataset
docs.text: External data source or sink referenced by a connector (e.g., S3 bucket, RDS table, REST API).

docs.title: Edge
docs.text: Connection between two nodes in the lineage graph. Edge types: PRODUCES, CONSUMES, TRANSFORMS, MATERIALIZES, HAS_SCHEMA, MEMBER_OF.

docs.title: Edge Type
docs.text: Semantic label for the relationship between nodes. See Edge Types section.

docs.title: Enrichment
docs.text: Process of backfilling node metadata from external APIs (e.g., Schema Registry, Stream Catalog, catalog providers).

docs.title: Environment
docs.text: Top-level organizational unit in Confluent Cloud. Contains clusters, connectors, Flink pools, and ksqlDB clusters.

docs.title: Extractor
docs.text: Client module that extracts lineage from a specific Confluent Cloud service. Examples: KafkaAdmin, Connect, Flink, ksqlDB.

docs.title: Flink
docs.text: Stream processing framework. LineageBridge extracts Flink SQL statements and parses them to identify input/output topics.

docs.title: Flink SQL
docs.text: SQL dialect for stream processing. LineageBridge parses CREATE TABLE, INSERT INTO, and CREATE TABLE AS SELECT statements.

docs.title: Glue Data Catalog
docs.text: See AWS Glue.

docs.title: Google Data Lineage
docs.text: Google Cloud service for tracking data lineage. LineageBridge provides a provider for publishing lineage to Google Data Lineage API.

docs.title: Graph
docs.text: NetworkX DiGraph instance that stores nodes and edges. See LineageGraph in models/graph.py.

docs.title: Kafka
docs.text: Distributed event streaming platform. Confluent Cloud is a fully managed Kafka service.

docs.title: KafkaAdmin
docs.text: LineageBridge client that extracts topic metadata and consumer group membership using the Kafka Admin API.

docs.title: ksqlDB
docs.text: Stream processing engine built on Kafka Streams. LineageBridge extracts persistent queries and parses SQL to identify lineage edges.

docs.title: Lineage
docs.text: Relationship between data assets showing how data flows and transforms across systems.

docs.title: Lineage Edge
docs.text: See Edge.

docs.title: Lineage Graph
docs.text: Complete directed graph of all extracted lineage. Stored as NetworkX DiGraph and serialized to JSON for UI rendering.

docs.title: Lineage Node
docs.text: See Node.

docs.title: MATERIALIZES
docs.text: Edge type connecting a Tableflow integration or topic to a catalog table (e.g., KAFKA_TOPIC -> MATERIALIZES -> UC_TABLE).

docs.title: Metrics API
docs.text: Confluent Cloud API for querying telemetry data (throughput, consumer lag). LineageBridge uses this to enrich nodes with real-time metrics.

docs.title: NetworkX
docs.text: Python library for graph analysis. LineageBridge uses networkx.DiGraph as the core data structure.

docs.title: Node
docs.text: Entity in the lineage graph representing a data asset (topic, connector, table, schema, etc.). See Node Types section.

docs.title: Node ID
docs.text: Unique identifier for a node with format: {system}:{type}:{env_id}:{qualified_name}. Example: CONFLUENT:KAFKA_TOPIC:env-abc123:my-topic.

docs.title: Node Type
docs.text: Category of data asset. Examples: KAFKA_TOPIC, CONNECTOR, UC_TABLE, FLINK_JOB, KSQLDB_QUERY.

docs.title: OpenLineage
docs.text: Open standard for data lineage metadata. LineageBridge exposes an OpenLineage-compatible API at /api/v1/lineage.

docs.title: Orchestrator
docs.text: Core extraction pipeline (extractors/orchestrator.py) that coordinates all clients in 5 phases.

docs.title: Protocol
docs.text: Python typing concept for structural subtyping. LineageBridge uses protocols for LineageExtractor and CatalogProvider to enable extensibility.

docs.title: Provider
docs.text: See Catalog Provider.

docs.title: Pydantic
docs.text: Python library for data validation. LineageBridge uses Pydantic v2 for all model definitions and settings.

docs.title: Pydantic Settings
docs.text: Pydantic module for loading configuration from environment variables. LineageBridge uses LINEAGE_BRIDGE_ prefix for all settings.

docs.title: Qualified Name
docs.text: Fully qualified identifier for a resource (e.g., catalog.schema.table for Unity Catalog, my-topic for Kafka topic).

docs.title: RBAC (Role-Based Access Control)
docs.text: Confluent Cloud authorization model. LineageBridge requires appropriate RBAC permissions to read metadata.

docs.title: REST v3
docs.text: Confluent Cloud REST API version 3. Primary API used by LineageBridge for extraction.

docs.title: SASL
docs.text: Simple Authentication and Security Layer. Protocol used for Kafka authentication. LineageBridge uses SASL/PLAIN for audit log consumer.

docs.title: Schema
docs.text: Data structure definition (Avro, Protobuf, JSON Schema) stored in Schema Registry.

docs.title: Schema Registry
docs.text: Confluent service for managing schemas. LineageBridge extracts schemas and creates HAS_SCHEMA edges to topics.

docs.title: SSL
docs.text: Secure Sockets Layer. LineageBridge uses SSL/TLS for all Kafka connections.

docs.title: Stream Catalog
docs.text: Confluent Cloud service for managing tags and business metadata on topics. LineageBridge extracts this metadata to enrich topic nodes.

docs.title: Streamlit
docs.text: Python framework for building data apps. LineageBridge UI is built with Streamlit.

docs.title: Sugiyama Layout
docs.text: Hierarchical graph layout algorithm that minimizes edge crossings. LineageBridge uses this to render DAGs top-to-bottom.

docs.title: System
docs.text: High-level categorization of nodes. Values: CONFLUENT, DATABRICKS, AWS, GOOGLE, EXTERNAL.

docs.title: Tableflow
docs.text: Confluent Cloud service that streams Kafka topics to external data catalogs (Unity Catalog, BigQuery, Snowflake). LineageBridge extracts Tableflow mappings to create MATERIALIZES edges.

docs.title: Topic
docs.text: Kafka topic where events are published and consumed. Central entity in stream lineage.

docs.title: UC (Unity Catalog)
docs.text: Databricks unified governance solution for data and AI assets. LineageBridge integrates with UC via the DatabricksUCProvider.

docs.title: Unity Catalog
docs.text: See UC.

docs.title: vis.js
docs.text: JavaScript library for network visualization. LineageBridge uses a custom Streamlit component wrapping vis.js for graph rendering.

docs.title: Watcher
docs.text: Change-detection service (watcher/engine.py) that polls Confluent APIs for changes and triggers re-extraction. Runs as a background thread or standalone CLI.

docs.title: Node Types
docs.text: Type Description System KAFKA_TOPIC Kafka topic CONFLUENT CONNECTOR Kafka Connect connector CONFLUENT KSQLDB_QUERY ksqlDB persistent query CONFLUENT FLINK_JOB Flink SQL statement CONFLUENT TABLEFLOW_TABLE Tableflow integration CONFLUENT UC_TABLE Unity Catalog table DATABRICKS GLUE_TABLE AWS Glue table AWS GOOGLE_DATASET Google BigQuery dataset GOOGLE SCHEMA Schema Registry schema CONFLUENT EXTERNAL_DATASET External data source/sink EXTERNAL CONSUMER_GROUP Kafka consumer group CONFLUENT

docs.title: Edge Types
docs.text: Type Description Example PRODUCES Source produces data to destination CONNECTOR -> PRODUCES -> KAFKA_TOPIC CONSUMES Source consumes data from destination CONNECTOR -> CONSUMES -> KAFKA_TOPIC TRANSFORMS Source transforms destination data KSQLDB_QUERY -> TRANSFORMS -> KAFKA_TOPIC MATERIALIZES Source materializes into destination catalog KAFKA_TOPIC -> MATERIALIZES -> UC_TABLE HAS_SCHEMA Topic has schema definition KAFKA_TOPIC -> HAS_SCHEMA -> SCHEMA MEMBER_OF Consumer is member of group CONSUMER -> MEMBER_OF -> CONSUMER_GROUP

docs.title: Acronyms
docs.text: Acronym Full Term ADR Architecture Decision Record API Application Programming Interface AWS Amazon Web Services BYOB Bring Your Own Bucket CI/CD Continuous Integration / Continuous Delivery CLI Command Line Interface DAG Directed Acyclic Graph GCP Google Cloud Platform HTTP Hypertext Transfer Protocol IAM Identity and Access Management JSON JavaScript Object Notation OL OpenLineage RBAC Role-Based Access Control RDS Relational Database Service REST Representational State Transfer SASL Simple Authentication and Security Layer SCIM System for Cross-domain Identity Management SQL Structured Query Language SR Schema Registry SSL Secure Sockets Layer TLS Transport Layer Security UC Unity Catalog UI User Interface URL Uniform Resource Locator

docs.title: Research Topics
docs.text: Exploratory work that may become features in future releases.

docs.title: Field-Level Lineage
docs.text: Track column-level transformations through SQL queries and schema mappings. Challenges: - Parsing complex SQL transformations (joins, aggregations, window functions) - Mapping fields across different schema formats (Avro ↔ Parquet ↔ Delta) - Handling schema evolution and breaking changes

docs.title: Real-Time Lineage Streaming
docs.text: Push lineage updates to a Kafka topic for downstream consumption. Use cases: - Real-time lineage indexing in search engines - Live lineage monitoring dashboards - Event-driven lineage validation pipelines

docs.title: AI-Powered Lineage Inference
docs.text: Use LLMs to infer missing lineage from code analysis: - Parse Kafka Streams applications for producer/consumer patterns - Analyze microservice code for topic dependencies - Suggest lineage connections based on naming conventions

docs.title: Lineage as Code
docs.text: Define expected lineage in declarative YAML/JSON files: - Validate actual lineage matches expected lineage - Detect drift and breaking changes - Version control lineage specifications

docs.title: Release Schedule
docs.text:
LineageBridge follows semantic versioning with roughly monthly minor releases and weekly patch releases as needed.

  • Patch releases (0.4.x): Bug fixes, documentation updates, minor improvements
  • Minor releases (0.x.0): New features, catalog providers, non-breaking changes
  • Major releases (x.0.0): Breaking API changes, architecture overhauls

docs.title: Deprecation Policy
docs.text:
Features marked for deprecation will be supported for at least one minor version before removal. Deprecation notices are included in:

  • Release notes in Changelog
  • Runtime warnings when deprecated features are used
  • Migration guides with recommended alternatives

docs.title: Troubleshooting Guide
docs.text: Quick reference for common issues and solutions when using LineageBridge.

docs.title: Quick Reference
docs.text: Issue Symptoms Solution Page 401 Unauthorized Extraction fails with "401" or "Unauthorized" Check API key/secret format, verify permissions Credential Issues 403 Forbidden Extraction fails with "403" or "Forbidden" Grant required permissions to API key Credential Issues 400 Bad Request Extraction fails with "400" or "Bad Request" Verify environment ID, check cluster access API Errors 429 Too Many Requests Extraction slow or fails with "429" Wait for rate limit reset, reduce concurrency API Errors 500/502/503/504 Errors Intermittent extraction failures Retry with exponential backoff (automatic) API Errors Timeout Extractor hangs or times out after 120s Check network, reduce scope, increase timeout Extraction Failures Missing topics Topics don't appear in graph Enable KafkaAdmin extractor, check cluster credentials Extraction Failures Missing connectors Connectors don't appear Enable Connect extractor, verify environment ID Extraction Failures Missing ksqlDB queries Queries don't appear Enable ksqlDB extractor, verify cluster credentials Extraction Failures Missing Flink jobs Flink statements don't appear Enable Flink extractor, verify organization ID Extraction Failures Missing schemas Schemas don't appear Enable Schema Registry extractor, verify SR endpoint Extraction Failures Missing catalog tables UC/Glue tables don't appear Enable Tableflow extractor, check catalog credentials Extraction Failures Orphan nodes Nodes with no edges in graph Expected in partial extractions, check enabled extractors Extraction Failures Slow extraction Extraction takes >5 minutes Reduce scope, disable metrics, optimize network Performance High memory usage UI crashes or freezes Filter graph, reduce node count, export subgraph Performance Watcher not detecting changes Changes not triggering re-extraction Check poll interval, verify watcher is running Performance

docs.title: First-Time Setup
docs.text:
1. Configure Cloud API key in .env: 

    LINEAGE_BRIDGE_CONFLUENT_CLOUD_API_KEY=your_cloud_api_key
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_CONFLUENT_CLOUD_API_SECRET=your_cloud_api_secret

  2. Test connectivity: 

    uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123

  3. If you see 401/403 errors, see Credential Issues.

docs.title: Debugging Missing Data
docs.text:
1. Check extractor status in UI sidebar — ensure all extractors are enabled.
  2. Review extraction logs for warnings: 
    uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 | grep "Warning"
  3. Verify API permissions in Confluent Cloud UI.
  4. See Extraction Failures for extractor-specific debugging.

docs.title: Performance Optimization
docs.text:
1. Disable unused extractors (metrics, stream catalog).
  2. Filter by specific cluster IDs: 
    uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 --cluster lkc-xyz789
  3. Export graph to JSON for offline analysis: 
    uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 --output graph.json
  4. See Performance for optimization tips.

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
• GitHub Issues: https://github.com/takabayashi/lineage-bridge/issues
  • Documentation: https://lineage-bridge.readthedocs.io
  • Logs: Check console output and .env for debug settings

docs.title: Pages
docs.text:
• Credential Issues - API key problems, 401/403 errors
  • API Errors - API error codes and solutions
  • Extraction Failures - Timeout, missing data issues
  • Performance - Optimization tips

docs.title: API Errors
docs.text: Common HTTP status codes and how LineageBridge handles them.

docs.title: Error Handling Strategy
docs.text:
LineageBridge uses a fail-safe extraction strategy:

  1. Retryable errors (429, 500, 502, 503, 504) - Automatic retry with exponential backoff
  2. Non-retryable errors (400, 401, 403, 404) - Log warning, return empty results, continue extraction
  3. Timeout errors - Log warning, return empty results, continue extraction

This ensures partial extraction succeeds even if some APIs fail.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The request was malformed or contained invalid parameters.

docs.title: Common Causes
docs.text:
1. Invalid environment ID or cluster ID
  2. Malformed query parameters
  3. Invalid JSON payload

docs.title: LineageBridge Behavior
docs.text:
Logs warning and returns empty results:

Extractor 'KafkaAdmin' got 400 Bad Request.
The API key may not have access to this environment,
or the API parameters are invalid.

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text:
1. Verify environment ID: 

    confluent environment list

  2. Verify cluster ID: 

    confluent kafka cluster list --environment env-abc123

  3. Check API key scope: 

    confluent api-key list

  4. Enable debug logging: 

    # .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The API key or token is missing, invalid, or expired.

docs.title: Common Causes
docs.text:
1. Missing credentials in .env
  2. Expired API key or token
  3. Wrong API key type (Cloud vs cluster-scoped)

docs.title: LineageBridge Behavior
docs.text:
Logs helpful error message:

Extractor 'KafkaAdmin:lkc-xyz789' got 401 Unauthorized.
This likely means a cluster-scoped API key is needed.
Set LINEAGE_BRIDGE_KAFKA_API_KEY in .env.

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text: See Credential Issues: 401 Unauthorized.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The API key lacks required permissions.

docs.title: Common Causes
docs.text:
1. Insufficient RBAC roles
  2. API key scoped to wrong resource
  3. Organization-level restrictions

docs.title: LineageBridge Behavior
docs.text:
Logs warning:

Extractor 'Connect' got 403 Forbidden. The API key lacks required permissions.

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text: See Credential Issues: 403 Forbidden.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The requested resource does not exist.

docs.title: Common Causes
docs.text:
1. Wrong environment ID or cluster ID
  2. Resource was deleted
  3. Wrong API endpoint URL

docs.title: LineageBridge Behavior
docs.text:
Logs warning and continues:

Extractor 'SchemaRegistry' got 404 Not Found.
Schema Registry may not be enabled in this environment.

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text:
1. Verify the resource exists: 

    confluent kafka cluster describe lkc-xyz789

  2. Check Schema Registry status: 

    confluent schema-registry cluster describe

  3. Manually set SR endpoint: 

    # .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_SCHEMA_REGISTRY_ENDPOINT=https://psrc-abc123.us-east-1.aws.confluent.cloud

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Rate limit exceeded. The client is sending too many requests.

docs.title: Common Causes
docs.text:
1. Excessive API calls in short time window
  2. Multiple concurrent extractions
  3. Organization-wide rate limit hit

docs.title: LineageBridge Behavior
docs.text:
Automatic retry with exponential backoff: - Retry 1: Wait 1 second - Retry 2: Wait 2 seconds - Retry 3: Wait 4 seconds - Respects Retry-After header if present

Log message:

Retryable status 429 for GET /kafka/v3/clusters (attempt 1/4)
Sleeping 1.0s before retry

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text:
1. Wait for rate limit to reset - Automatic with retries
  2. Reduce concurrent extractors - Disable unused extractors in UI
  3. Increase retry backoff - Not currently configurable
  4. Use caching - Enable graph caching: 
    # .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_CACHE_ENABLED=true

docs.title: Rate Limit Details
docs.text: Confluent Cloud API rate limits (approximate): - Cloud API: 1000 requests/hour per API key - Kafka REST: 1000 requests/hour per cluster - Schema Registry: 1000 requests/hour per cluster

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The API server encountered an unexpected condition.

docs.title: Common Causes
docs.text:
1. Transient server error
  2. Backend service outage
  3. Malformed data in upstream system

docs.title: LineageBridge Behavior
docs.text:
Automatic retry with exponential backoff (same as 429).

Log message:

Retryable status 500 for GET /connect/v1/environments/env-abc123/clusters (attempt 1/4)

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text:
1. Wait for automatic retry - LineageBridge retries up to 3 times
  2. Check Confluent Cloud status - https://status.confluent.cloud
  3. Try again later - If retries fail, wait a few minutes and re-run extraction
  4. Report persistent errors - If error persists >1 hour, contact Confluent support

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The API gateway received an invalid response from upstream.

docs.title: Common Causes
docs.text:
1. Backend service restart or deployment
  2. Network issues between gateway and backend
  3. Timeout in upstream service

docs.title: LineageBridge Behavior
docs.text: Automatic retry (same as 500).

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text: Same as 500 Internal Server Error.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The API server is temporarily unable to handle requests.

docs.title: Common Causes
docs.text:
1. Planned maintenance
  2. Overloaded backend
  3. Resource exhaustion

docs.title: LineageBridge Behavior
docs.text:
Automatic retry (same as 500).

Log message:

Retryable status 503 for POST /ksqldb/v2/clusters/lksqlc-abc/ksql (attempt 2/4)
Sleeping 2.0s before retry

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text:
1. Check maintenance windows - Confluent Cloud status page
  2. Retry with backoff - Automatic
  3. Reduce load - Disable heavy extractors (metrics, stream catalog)

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The API gateway timed out waiting for upstream response.

docs.title: Common Causes
docs.text:
1. Long-running query in backend
  2. Database query timeout
  3. Network latency

docs.title: LineageBridge Behavior
docs.text: Automatic retry (same as 500).

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text:
1. Reduce query scope - Filter by specific cluster: 

    uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 --cluster lkc-xyz789

  2. Increase timeout - Not currently configurable (30s per request, 120s per extractor)

  3. Split extraction - Extract clusters separately and merge graphs

docs.title: Description
docs.text: LineageBridge timed out waiting for API response.

docs.title: Timeouts
docs.text:
• Per-request timeout: 30 seconds (httpx client)
  • Per-extractor timeout: 120 seconds (orchestrator)

docs.title: Common Causes
docs.text:
1. Slow network connection
  2. Large response payload (1000s of resources)
  3. Overloaded API server

docs.title: LineageBridge Behavior
docs.text:
Logs warning and continues:

Extractor 'KafkaAdmin:lkc-xyz789' timed out after 120s

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text:
1. Check network connectivity: 

    ping api.confluent.cloud
curl -I https://api.confluent.cloud

  2. Reduce extraction scope: 

    uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 --cluster lkc-xyz789

  3. Disable slow extractors:

  4. Metrics (optional, can be slow for large clusters)

  5. Stream Catalog (optional)

  6. Extract in stages: 

    # Extract without enrichment
uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 --no-enrich

# Enrich separately
uv run lineage-bridge-extract --enrich-only

docs.title: Default Policy
docs.text:
_MAX_RETRIES = 3
_BACKOFF_BASE = 1.0  # seconds
_RETRYABLE_STATUS_CODES = {429, 500, 502, 503, 504}

Backoff delay: 1.0 * (2 ** attempt) seconds (1s, 2s, 4s)

docs.title: Customization
docs.text:
Retry configuration is not currently exposed via environment variables. To customize:

  1. Edit lineage_bridge/clients/base.py: 

    _MAX_RETRIES = 5  # Increase retries
_BACKOFF_BASE = 2.0  # Slower backoff

  2. Or create a custom client class extending ConfluentClient.

docs.title: Error Recovery Workflow
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[API Request] --> B{HTTP Status}
    B -->|200-299| C[Success]
    B -->|429, 5xx| D[Retryable Error]
    B -->|400, 401, 403, 404| E[Non-Retryable Error]
    B -->|Timeout| F[Timeout Error]

    D --> G{Retry Count < 3?}
    G -->|Yes| H[Exponential Backoff]
    H --> A
    G -->|No| I[Log Warning]

    E --> I
    F --> I

    I --> J[Return Empty Results]
    J --> K[Continue Extraction]

    C --> L[Return Results]
    L --> K

docs.title: Enable Debug Logging
docs.text:
# .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG

This logs all HTTP requests and responses:

DEBUG Request GET /kafka/v3/clusters params={'environment': 'env-abc123'} attempt=1
DEBUG Response 200 GET /kafka/v3/clusters (0.234s)

docs.title: Inspect Raw Responses
docs.text:
Use curl to test API endpoints directly:

# Cloud API
curl -u "$LINEAGE_BRIDGE_CONFLUENT_CLOUD_API_KEY:$LINEAGE_BRIDGE_CONFLUENT_CLOUD_API_SECRET" \
  "https://api.confluent.cloud/org/v2/environments"

# Kafka REST API
curl -u "$LINEAGE_BRIDGE_KAFKA_API_KEY:$LINEAGE_BRIDGE_KAFKA_API_SECRET" \
  "https://pkc-abc123.us-east-1.aws.confluent.cloud:443/kafka/v3/clusters/lkc-xyz789/topics"

docs.title: Check API Status
docs.text:
• Confluent Cloud: https://status.confluent.cloud
  • Databricks: https://status.databricks.com
  • AWS: https://status.aws.amazon.com
  • Google Cloud: https://status.cloud.google.com

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Credential Issues - 401/403 error solutions
  • Extraction Failures - Missing data debugging
  • Performance - Timeout and slowness optimization

docs.title: Credential Issues
docs.text: Common authentication and authorization problems when using LineageBridge.

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
Extractor 'KafkaAdmin:lkc-xyz789' got 401 Unauthorized.
This likely means a cluster-scoped API key is needed.
Set LINEAGE_BRIDGE_KAFKA_API_KEY in .env.

docs.title: Causes
docs.text:
1. Missing API key - No credentials configured
  2. Invalid API key - Typo, expired, or deleted key
  3. Wrong API key type - Cloud API key used where cluster-scoped key is required

docs.title: For Confluent Cloud API
docs.text:
Verify your Cloud API key is correctly configured:

# .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_CONFLUENT_CLOUD_API_KEY=ABCDEF1234567890
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_CONFLUENT_CLOUD_API_SECRET=XYZABC...

Test the key with the Confluent CLI:

confluent login --save
confluent environment list

docs.title: For Cluster-Scoped Operations
docs.text:
Some operations (Kafka REST API) require cluster-scoped API keys:

# .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_KAFKA_API_KEY=CLUSTER_KEY
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_KAFKA_API_SECRET=CLUSTER_SECRET

Create a cluster API key:

confluent api-key create --resource lkc-xyz789

docs.title: For Schema Registry
docs.text:
Schema Registry has its own API keys:

# .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_SCHEMA_REGISTRY_API_KEY=SR_KEY
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_SCHEMA_REGISTRY_API_SECRET=SR_SECRET

If not set, LineageBridge will try the Cloud API key, which may not work.

Create a Schema Registry API key:

confluent api-key create --resource lsrc-abc123

docs.title: For ksqlDB
docs.text:
ksqlDB clusters can have their own API keys:

# .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_KSQLDB_API_KEY=KSQL_KEY
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_KSQLDB_API_SECRET=KSQL_SECRET

Create a ksqlDB API key:

confluent api-key create --resource lksqlc-xyz789

docs.title: For Flink
docs.text:
Flink SQL has its own API keys:

# .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_FLINK_API_KEY=FLINK_KEY
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_FLINK_API_SECRET=FLINK_SECRET

Create a Flink API key in the Confluent Cloud UI.

docs.title: For Databricks
docs.text:
Databricks uses personal access tokens:

# .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_DATABRICKS_WORKSPACE_URL=https://myworkspace.cloud.databricks.com
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_DATABRICKS_TOKEN=dapi1234567890abcdef

Create a token in Databricks: 1. User Settings → Developer → Access Tokens → Generate New Token

docs.title: For AWS Glue
docs.text:
AWS Glue uses boto3 credential chain (environment variables, ~/.aws/credentials, IAM roles):

# .env or environment
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY
AWS_REGION=us-east-1

Or configure AWS CLI:

aws configure

docs.title: For Google BigQuery
docs.text:
Google uses Application Default Credentials:

# .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_GCP_PROJECT_ID=my-project
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_GCP_LOCATION=us-central1

Authenticate with gcloud:

gcloud auth application-default login

Or set service account key:

export GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=/path/to/key.json

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text: Extractor 'Connect' got 403 Forbidden. The API key lacks required permissions.

docs.title: Causes
docs.text:
1. Insufficient permissions - API key doesn't have required RBAC roles
  2. Wrong resource scope - Key scoped to wrong cluster/environment
  3. Organization restrictions - API disabled at organization level

docs.title: Verify Cloud API Key Permissions
docs.text:
Required permissions for LineageBridge:

  • CloudClusterAdmin or CloudClusterRead - Read cluster metadata
  • ResourceOwner or ResourceRead - Read connectors, ksqlDB, Flink
  • OrganizationAdmin (optional) - For organization-level discovery

Grant permissions in Confluent Cloud UI: 1. Accounts & Access → Service Accounts 2. Select the service account 3. Add role bindings

docs.title: Verify Cluster API Key Permissions
docs.text: Cluster API keys need: - Read permission on the cluster

docs.title: Verify Schema Registry Permissions
docs.text: Schema Registry keys need: - Read permission on Schema Registry cluster

docs.title: Verify Databricks Permissions
docs.text:
Databricks tokens need: - CAN_USE on SQL Warehouse (for Tableflow) - SELECT on catalog.schema.table (for enrichment) - MODIFY on catalog.schema.table (for lineage push)

Grant permissions in Databricks:

GRANT USE SCHEMA ON CATALOG main TO `user@example.com`;
GRANT SELECT ON TABLE main.sales.orders TO `user@example.com`;

docs.title: Verify AWS Glue Permissions
docs.text:
AWS IAM user/role needs: - glue:GetTable - Read table metadata - glue:UpdateTable - Write lineage metadata

Example IAM policy:

{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "glue:GetTable",
        "glue:UpdateTable",
        "glue:GetDatabase",
        "glue:GetDatabases",
        "glue:GetTables"
      ],
      "Resource": "*"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Verify Google BigQuery Permissions
docs.text:
Service account or user needs: - bigquery.tables.get - Read table metadata - datalineage.events.create - Push lineage events

Grant permissions in Google Cloud Console: 1. IAM & Admin → IAM 2. Add role: BigQuery Data Viewer + Data Lineage Producer

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
Extractor 'KafkaAdmin' got 400 Bad Request.
The API key may not have access to this environment,
or the API parameters are invalid.

docs.title: Causes
docs.text:
1. Invalid environment ID - Typo or environment doesn't exist
  2. Invalid cluster ID - Cluster doesn't exist or wrong environment
  3. Malformed request - Bug in client code (rare)

docs.title: Verify Environment ID
docs.text:
List environments with the Confluent CLI:

confluent environment list

Use the correct ID format:

uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123  # Correct
uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env abc123      # Wrong

docs.title: Verify Cluster ID
docs.text:
List clusters in the environment:

confluent kafka cluster list --environment env-abc123

Filter by specific cluster:

uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 --cluster lkc-xyz789

docs.title: Verify Resource Scope
docs.text:
Ensure the API key is scoped to the correct environment:

confluent api-key list

Output should show the environment association.

docs.title: Confluent Cloud
docs.text:
# Required
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_CONFLUENT_CLOUD_API_KEY=your_cloud_api_key
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_CONFLUENT_CLOUD_API_SECRET=your_cloud_api_secret

# Optional (fall back to Cloud API key)
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_KAFKA_API_KEY=cluster_api_key
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_KAFKA_API_SECRET=cluster_api_secret
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_SCHEMA_REGISTRY_API_KEY=sr_api_key
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_SCHEMA_REGISTRY_API_SECRET=sr_api_secret
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_KSQLDB_API_KEY=ksql_api_key
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_KSQLDB_API_SECRET=ksql_api_secret
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_FLINK_API_KEY=flink_api_key
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_FLINK_API_SECRET=flink_api_secret
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_TABLEFLOW_API_KEY=tableflow_api_key
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_TABLEFLOW_API_SECRET=tableflow_api_secret

docs.title: Databricks
docs.text:
# Required for UC enrichment and lineage push
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_DATABRICKS_WORKSPACE_URL=https://myworkspace.cloud.databricks.com
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_DATABRICKS_TOKEN=dapi1234567890abcdef

# Optional (auto-discovered)
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_DATABRICKS_WAREHOUSE_ID=abc123def456

docs.title: AWS
docs.text:
# AWS region (required for Glue)
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_AWS_REGION=us-east-1

# AWS credentials (use environment or ~/.aws/credentials)
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY

docs.title: Google Cloud
docs.text:
# GCP project and location (required for BigQuery/Data Lineage)
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_GCP_PROJECT_ID=my-project
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_GCP_LOCATION=us-central1

# Credentials (use gcloud or service account key)
GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=/path/to/key.json

docs.title: Quick Test
docs.text:
uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123

Look for these log messages: - ✓ Discovered N clusters in env-abc123 - Cloud API key works - ✓ Schema Registry found: https://... - SR discovery works - ✓ Phase 1/4: Extracting Kafka topics - Cluster access works

docs.title: Per-Service Tests
docs.text:
Confluent Cloud: 

confluent environment list
confluent kafka cluster list --environment env-abc123

Schema Registry: 

curl -u "$LINEAGE_BRIDGE_SCHEMA_REGISTRY_API_KEY:$LINEAGE_BRIDGE_SCHEMA_REGISTRY_API_SECRET" \
  https://psrc-abc123.us-east-1.aws.confluent.cloud/subjects

Databricks: 

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $LINEAGE_BRIDGE_DATABRICKS_TOKEN" \
  "$LINEAGE_BRIDGE_DATABRICKS_WORKSPACE_URL/api/2.0/sql/warehouses"

AWS Glue: 

aws glue get-databases --region us-east-1

Google BigQuery: 

bq ls $LINEAGE_BRIDGE_GCP_PROJECT_ID:

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• API Errors - Handling API error codes
  • Extraction Failures - Debugging missing data
  • Getting Started: Configuration

docs.title: Extraction Failures
docs.text: Debugging common issues when extraction succeeds but produces incomplete or unexpected results.

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text: Extractor 'KafkaAdmin:lkc-xyz789' timed out after 120s

docs.title: Causes
docs.text:
1. Slow API response - Large clusters, many topics
  2. Network latency - Distance from API region, poor connectivity
  3. Overloaded backend - Confluent Cloud API under load

docs.title: Reduce Extraction Scope
docs.text:
Extract one cluster at a time:

uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 --cluster lkc-xyz789

docs.title: Disable Heavy Extractors
docs.text:
Metrics and Stream Catalog can be slow:

# In UI: uncheck "Metrics" and "Stream Catalog" in sidebar

# Or disable via orchestrator in code
await run_extraction(
    settings,
    environment_ids=["env-abc123"],
    enable_metrics=False,
    enable_stream_catalog=False,
)

docs.title: Split Extraction and Enrichment
docs.text:
# Extract without enrichment
uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 --no-enrich --output graph_raw.json

# Enrich separately
uv run lineage-bridge-extract --enrich-only --output graph_raw.json

docs.title: Check Network
docs.text:
Test latency to API endpoints:

ping api.confluent.cloud
curl -w "@-" -o /dev/null -s https://api.confluent.cloud <<'EOF'
     time_namelookup:  %{time_namelookup}s\n
        time_connect:  %{time_connect}s\n
     time_appconnect:  %{time_appconnect}s\n
       time_redirect:  %{time_redirect}s\n
    time_pretransfer:  %{time_pretransfer}s\n
  time_starttransfer:  %{time_starttransfer}s\n
                     ----------\n
          time_total:  %{time_total}s\n
EOF

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• Graph shows no topics
  • Graph shows some topics but not all
  • Specific topics are missing

docs.title: Causes
docs.text:
1. KafkaAdmin extractor disabled - Extractor not running
  2. Wrong cluster credentials - Using Cloud API key instead of cluster key
  3. Cluster filter mismatch - Topic exists in different cluster
  4. Permission issues - API key lacks read permissions

docs.title: Enable KafkaAdmin Extractor
docs.text:
Check in UI sidebar or CLI:

await run_extraction(
    settings,
    environment_ids=["env-abc123"],
    enable_kafka_admin=True,  # Ensure this is True
)

docs.title: Verify Cluster Credentials
docs.text:
Some clusters require cluster-scoped API keys:

# .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_KAFKA_API_KEY=CLUSTER_KEY
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_KAFKA_API_SECRET=CLUSTER_SECRET

Create a cluster API key:

confluent api-key create --resource lkc-xyz789

docs.title: Remove Cluster Filter
docs.text:
If using --cluster, remove it to see all clusters:

uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123  # No --cluster flag

docs.title: Check Permissions
docs.text:
Verify API key has read permissions:

confluent api-key list

Test with Confluent CLI:

confluent kafka topic list --cluster lkc-xyz789

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• Graph shows topics but no connectors
  • Some connectors are missing

docs.title: Causes
docs.text:
1. Connect extractor disabled
  2. Wrong environment ID
  3. Connector in different cluster

docs.title: Enable Connect Extractor
docs.text:
await run_extraction(
    settings,
    environment_ids=["env-abc123"],
    enable_connect=True,
)

docs.title: Verify Environment
docs.text:
List connectors in environment:

confluent connect cluster list --environment env-abc123
confluent connect list --cluster lcc-abc123

docs.title: Check Connector Status
docs.text:
Connector may be FAILED or DELETED:

confluent connect describe <connector-name> --cluster lcc-abc123

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• Graph shows topics but no ksqlDB queries
  • Some queries are missing

docs.title: Causes
docs.text:
1. ksqlDB extractor disabled
  2. Wrong ksqlDB credentials
  3. ksqlDB cluster not found
  4. Query is not persistent (only persistent queries are extracted)

docs.title: Enable ksqlDB Extractor
docs.text:
await run_extraction(
    settings,
    environment_ids=["env-abc123"],
    enable_ksqldb=True,
)

docs.title: Verify ksqlDB Cluster
docs.text:
List ksqlDB clusters:

confluent ksql app list --environment env-abc123

docs.title: Check Query Type
docs.text:
Only persistent queries are extracted (CTAS, CSAS):

-- This is extracted
CREATE STREAM enriched_orders AS
  SELECT * FROM orders WHERE amount > 100;

-- This is NOT extracted (transient query)
SELECT * FROM orders;

List persistent queries:

confluent ksql app describe lksqlc-abc123

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• Graph shows topics but no Flink statements
  • Some statements are missing

docs.title: Causes
docs.text:
1. Flink extractor disabled
  2. Wrong organization ID
  3. Flink credentials missing
  4. Statement is STOPPED or DELETED

docs.title: Enable Flink Extractor
docs.text:
await run_extraction(
    settings,
    environment_ids=["env-abc123"],
    enable_flink=True,
)

docs.title: Verify Organization ID
docs.text:
Flink API requires organization ID:

# Check logs for organization ID
uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 | grep "organization"

If missing:

Could not determine organization ID for Flink in env-abc123 — Flink extraction skipped

Organization ID is auto-discovered from cluster metadata. If this fails, it's a bug — please report it.

docs.title: Check Statement Status
docs.text:
Only RUNNING statements are extracted:

confluent flink statement list --environment env-abc123

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• Graph shows topics but no schema nodes
  • HAS_SCHEMA edges are missing
  • Schema enrichment skipped

docs.title: Causes
docs.text:
1. Schema Registry extractor disabled
  2. Schema Registry endpoint not found
  3. Schema Registry credentials missing
  4. Topics don't use schemas (e.g., JSON, Avro with embedded schema)

docs.title: Enable Schema Registry Extractor
docs.text:
await run_extraction(
    settings,
    environment_ids=["env-abc123"],
    enable_schema_registry=True,
)

docs.title: Verify Schema Registry Endpoint
docs.text:
Check logs for:

Schema Registry found: https://psrc-abc123.us-east-1.aws.confluent.cloud

If not found:

No Schema Registry endpoint found for env-abc123.
Set LINEAGE_BRIDGE_SCHEMA_REGISTRY_API_KEY in .env
or check Stream Governance is enabled.

Manually set endpoint:

# .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_SCHEMA_REGISTRY_ENDPOINT=https://psrc-abc123.us-east-1.aws.confluent.cloud
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_SCHEMA_REGISTRY_API_KEY=SR_KEY
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_SCHEMA_REGISTRY_API_SECRET=SR_SECRET

docs.title: Check Schema Exists
docs.text:
List schemas:

confluent schema-registry schema list --environment env-abc123

Topics without schemas won't have HAS_SCHEMA edges.

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• Graph shows topics but no UC/Glue/BigQuery tables
  • MATERIALIZES edges are missing
  • Tableflow extraction skipped

docs.title: Causes
docs.text:
1. Tableflow extractor disabled
  2. No Tableflow integrations configured
  3. Catalog credentials missing
  4. Table not mapped to topic

docs.title: Enable Tableflow Extractor
docs.text:
await run_extraction(
    settings,
    environment_ids=["env-abc123"],
    enable_tableflow=True,
)

docs.title: Verify Tableflow Integrations
docs.text:
Check if Tableflow is configured in Confluent Cloud:

  1. Confluent Cloud UI → Environment → Tableflow
  2. Verify integrations exist (Unity Catalog, AWS Glue, etc.)
  3. Verify topic-table mappings exist

docs.title: Verify Catalog Credentials
docs.text:
Databricks UC: 

# .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_DATABRICKS_WORKSPACE_URL=https://myworkspace.cloud.databricks.com
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_DATABRICKS_TOKEN=dapi...

AWS Glue: 

# .env or ~/.aws/credentials
AWS_REGION=us-east-1
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=...
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=...

Google BigQuery: 

# .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_GCP_PROJECT_ID=my-project
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_GCP_LOCATION=us-central1

Test credentials:

# Databricks
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $LINEAGE_BRIDGE_DATABRICKS_TOKEN" \
  "$LINEAGE_BRIDGE_DATABRICKS_WORKSPACE_URL/api/2.0/sql/warehouses"

# AWS
aws glue get-databases --region us-east-1

# Google
bq ls $LINEAGE_BRIDGE_GCP_PROJECT_ID:

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
Graph validation warnings:

Orphan node: confluent:kafka_topic:env-abc123:my-topic

docs.title: Causes
docs.text:
1. Topic has no producers or consumers
  2. Producers/consumers in disabled extractors
  3. Expected behavior - Not all topics have lineage

docs.title: Enable All Extractors
docs.text:
Orphan topics may have producers/consumers in disabled extractors:

await run_extraction(
    settings,
    environment_ids=["env-abc123"],
    enable_connect=True,
    enable_ksqldb=True,
    enable_flink=True,
)

docs.title: Check Topic Usage
docs.text:
Topic may genuinely have no producers or consumers:

# Check consumer groups
confluent kafka topic describe my-topic --cluster lkc-xyz789

# Check connectors
confluent connect list --cluster lcc-abc123

docs.title: Ignore Warnings
docs.text:
Orphan nodes are expected in partial extractions. The graph is still valid and usable.

To hide orphans in UI, use the filter:

UI → Filters → Hide orphan nodes

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
Graph validation warnings:

Dangling edge src: confluent:connector:env-abc123:lkc-xyz789:mysql-source
Dangling edge dst: confluent:kafka_topic:env-abc123:orders

docs.title: Causes
docs.text:
1. Bug in extractor - Edge references non-existent node
  2. Race condition - Node deleted between extraction phases
  3. Cluster filter - Edge references node in filtered-out cluster

docs.title: Enable Full Extraction
docs.text:
Remove cluster filters:

uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123  # No --cluster flag

docs.title: Report Bug
docs.text:
Dangling edges should not occur. If you see this consistently, it's a bug:

  1. Export graph: graph.to_json_file("graph.json")
  2. Open GitHub issue with graph JSON and extraction logs

docs.title: Workaround
docs.text: Graph is still usable. Dangling edges are ignored in rendering.

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• Some extractors return empty results
  • Graph is smaller than expected
  • Multiple warnings in logs

docs.title: Causes
docs.text:
1. Extractor failures - API errors, timeouts
  2. Missing credentials - Some extractors skipped
  3. Resource not configured - No connectors, ksqlDB, etc.

docs.title: Review Logs
docs.text:
Look for warnings:

uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 | grep "Warning"

Common warnings:

Warning: No Schema Registry endpoint found
Warning: Extractor 'Connect' failed: 403 Forbidden
Warning: Could not determine organization ID for Flink

docs.title: Enable Debug Logging
docs.text:
# .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG

docs.title: Extract in Stages
docs.text:
Extract different resource types separately to isolate failures:

# Extract topics only
await run_extraction(
    settings,
    environment_ids=["env-abc123"],
    enable_connect=False,
    enable_ksqldb=False,
    enable_flink=False,
    enable_schema_registry=False,
)

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• API Errors - HTTP error codes
  • Credential Issues - Authentication problems
  • Performance - Optimization tips

docs.title: Performance Optimization
docs.text: Tips for improving extraction speed, reducing memory usage, and troubleshooting watcher issues.

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• Extraction takes >5 minutes
  • Specific phases are slow (check logs for timing)
  • UI becomes unresponsive during extraction

docs.title: Performance Characteristics
docs.text:
Typical extraction time: - Small environment (1 cluster, 10 topics): ~10s - Medium environment (3 clusters, 100 topics): ~30s - Large environment (10 clusters, 1000 topics): ~2-3 min

If extraction is significantly slower, optimization may help.

docs.title: Disable Unused Extractors
docs.text:
Metrics and Stream Catalog add overhead:

await run_extraction(
    settings,
    environment_ids=["env-abc123"],
    enable_metrics=False,          # Optional, can be slow
    enable_stream_catalog=False,   # Optional
)

In UI, uncheck these in the sidebar.

docs.title: Reduce Extraction Scope
docs.text:
Extract one cluster at a time:

uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 --cluster lkc-xyz789

docs.title: Skip Enrichment
docs.text:
Extract without catalog enrichment:

uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 --no-enrich

Enrich later:

uv run lineage-bridge-extract --enrich-only --output lineage_graph.json

docs.title: Reduce Metrics Lookback
docs.text:
Metrics lookback default is 1 hour. Reduce it:

await run_extraction(
    settings,
    environment_ids=["env-abc123"],
    enable_metrics=True,
    metrics_lookback_hours=0.25,  # 15 minutes
)

docs.title: Use Caching
docs.text:
Cache extraction results to avoid re-fetching:

# Export to JSON
uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 --output graph.json

# Load from JSON in UI
# (Not currently supported in UI, use CLI)

docs.title: Optimize Network
docs.text:
• Run extraction from same region as Confluent Cloud environment
  • Use wired connection instead of WiFi
  • Check for VPN overhead

Test network latency:

curl -w "time_total: %{time_total}s\n" -o /dev/null -s https://api.confluent.cloud

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• UI crashes or freezes
  • Browser tab becomes unresponsive
  • Python process uses >2GB RAM

docs.title: Memory Characteristics
docs.text:
Approximate memory usage: - 100 nodes: ~50MB - 1,000 nodes: ~200MB - 10,000 nodes: ~1GB

Graph rendering in UI (vis.js) adds overhead.

docs.title: Filter Graph
docs.text: Use filters in UI sidebar: - Filter by node type (e.g., only topics and connectors) - Filter by environment/cluster - Hide orphan nodes - Search for specific nodes

docs.title: Export Subgraph
docs.text:
Extract a subset of the graph:

# Filter by cluster
graph_filtered = LineageGraph()
for node in graph.nodes:
    if node.cluster_id == "lkc-xyz789":
        graph_filtered.add_node(node)

for edge in graph.edges:
    if edge.src_id in graph_filtered._nodes and edge.dst_id in graph_filtered._nodes:
        graph_filtered.add_edge(edge)

graph_filtered.to_json_file("graph_filtered.json")

docs.title: Reduce Node Count
docs.text:
• Extract fewer environments/clusters
  • Disable heavy extractors (connectors, schemas)
  • Use cluster filter

docs.title: Increase Browser Memory
docs.text:
Chrome/Edge:

google-chrome --js-flags="--max-old-space-size=4096"

Firefox: about:config → javascript.options.mem.max → 4096

docs.title: Use CLI Instead of UI
docs.text:
For large graphs, use CLI and export to JSON:

uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 --output graph.json

Analyze JSON with scripts or tools like jq.

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• Watcher running but changes not triggering re-extraction
  • Changes take >1 minute to reflect

docs.title: Causes
docs.text:
1. Poll interval too long - Default 10s
  2. Debounce cooldown - 30s cooldown after last change
  3. Change not in polled resources - Watcher only polls topics, connectors, ksqlDB, Flink

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text:
Verify watcher is running:

# CLI
uv run lineage-bridge-watch

# UI
Check sidebar: "Watcher" toggle should be ON

Check logs:

INFO ChangePoller detected change: topics in cluster lkc-xyz789
INFO WatcherEngine triggered extraction due to changes

Understand debounce:

Watcher waits 30s after last change before extracting. This batches rapid changes.

10:00:00 - Change detected: topic created
10:00:05 - Change detected: connector created
10:00:35 - Extraction triggered (30s after last change)

Manually trigger extraction:

In UI, click "Extract" button in sidebar.

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• High CPU usage when watcher is running
  • Python process uses 100% CPU

docs.title: Causes
docs.text:
1. Poll interval too short - Default 10s is reasonable
  2. Large number of resources - Polling 1000s of topics
  3. API throttling - Hitting rate limits

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text:
Increase poll interval:

Edit watcher/engine.py:

_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS = 30  # Increase from 10

Reduce scope:

Watcher polls all clusters in all configured environments. To watch fewer resources:

# .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_WATCHER_ENVIRONMENTS=env-abc123  # Comma-separated
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_WATCHER_CLUSTERS=lkc-xyz789      # Comma-separated

(Not currently implemented, but can be added.)

Disable watcher:

If not needed, turn off watcher:

# UI: Toggle off in sidebar
# CLI: Don't run lineage-bridge-watch

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• Watcher toggle stuck in ON state
  • Python thread won't stop

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text:
Kill watcher thread:

In UI, refresh the page. Watcher runs in background thread and will stop on page refresh.

In CLI, Ctrl+C stops the watcher.

Check for hung processes:

ps aux | grep lineage-bridge-watch
kill -9 <PID>

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• UI freezes when rendering graph
  • Graph layout takes >10 seconds
  • Pan/zoom is laggy

docs.title: Use Hierarchical Layout
docs.text:
Hierarchical layout (Sugiyama) is faster than force-directed:

# Default in UI
layout = "hierarchical"

docs.title: Reduce Node Count
docs.text: Filter graph before rendering (see High Memory Usage).

docs.title: Disable Physics
docs.text:
For large graphs, disable physics simulation:

// In visjs_graph component
physics: {
  enabled: false
}

(Requires custom component modification.)

docs.title: Export to Image
docs.text:
For static analysis, export graph to PNG:

# Not currently supported, but can be added with networkx
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import networkx as nx

nx.draw(graph._graph, with_labels=True)
plt.savefig("graph.png")

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• Extraction slow due to 429 errors
  • Many retry warnings in logs

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text: See API Errors: 429 Too Many Requests.

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• Catalog enrichment slow (Phase 4b)
  • Databricks/Glue API calls timing out

docs.title: Skip Enrichment
docs.text: uv run lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123 --no-enrich

docs.title: Optimize Databricks
docs.text:
• Use serverless SQL warehouse (faster cold start)
  • Ensure warehouse is RUNNING before extraction
# .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_DATABRICKS_WAREHOUSE_ID=abc123def456  # Pre-warm warehouse

docs.title: Optimize AWS Glue
docs.text:
• Use same region as LineageBridge
  • Increase AWS CLI timeout:
# ~/.aws/config
[default]
cli_read_timeout = 60

docs.title: Optimize Google BigQuery
docs.text:
• Use same region as LineageBridge
  • Increase ADC timeout (not currently configurable)

docs.title: Enable Performance Logging
docs.text:
# .env
LINEAGE_BRIDGE_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG

This logs timing for each phase:

INFO Phase 1/4 complete: 12.3s
INFO Phase 2/4 complete: 5.6s
INFO Phase 3/4 complete: 3.2s
INFO Phase 4/4 complete: 8.1s

docs.title: Profile Python Code
docs.text:
Use cProfile to profile extraction:

python -m cProfile -o extraction.prof -m lineage_bridge.extractors.orchestrator

# Analyze profile
python -m pstats extraction.prof

docs.title: Measure API Latency
docs.text:
Use httpx logging:

import logging
logging.getLogger("httpx").setLevel(logging.DEBUG)

This logs request/response times:

DEBUG HTTP Request: GET https://api.confluent.cloud/... "HTTP/1.1 200 OK"

docs.title: Python
docs.text:
• Max graph size: ~100K nodes (limited by memory)
  • Max file size: ~100MB JSON (limited by disk I/O)
  • Max concurrent requests: Limited by httpx (default 100 connections)

docs.title: Browser (UI)
docs.text:
• Max nodes rendered: ~5,000 (limited by vis.js performance)
  • Max memory: ~2GB (limited by browser tab)

docs.title: Confluent Cloud API
docs.text:
• Rate limit: ~1000 requests/hour per API key
  • Page size: 100 items per page (default)
  • Response timeout: 30s per request

docs.title: Optimization Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Disable unused extractors (metrics, stream catalog)
  • [ ] Filter by specific cluster IDs
  • [ ] Skip enrichment (--no-enrich)
  • [ ] Reduce metrics lookback window
  • [ ] Use same region as Confluent Cloud
  • [ ] Filter graph in UI before rendering
  • [ ] Export subgraph for large datasets
  • [ ] Check API rate limits
  • [ ] Enable debug logging to identify bottlenecks

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Extraction Failures - Debugging incomplete results
  • API Errors - Handling rate limits and retries
  • Architecture: Extraction Pipeline - Understanding phases

docs.title: User Guide
docs.text: Welcome to the LineageBridge User Guide. This section covers all user-facing tools and interfaces for working with LineageBridge.

docs.title: Tools Overview
docs.text: LineageBridge provides four main user-facing tools:

docs.title: CLI Tools
docs.text: Tool Purpose Best For lineage-bridge-extract Extract lineage from Confluent Cloud One-time exports, automation, CI/CD lineage-bridge-watch Monitor for changes and auto-extract Keeping lineage up-to-date in production lineage-bridge-ui Interactive Streamlit application Visual exploration, discovery, analysis lineage-bridge-api REST API server Integration with other tools, programmatic access

docs.title: Streamlit UI
docs.text:
The Streamlit UI is the primary interactive interface for LineageBridge. It provides:

  • Visual graph exploration with drag, zoom, and search
  • Environment and cluster discovery
  • Configurable extraction with progress tracking
  • Change detection watcher controls
  • Node inspection with deep links
  • Export and load capabilities

See the Streamlit UI Guide for a complete walkthrough.

docs.title: Graph Visualization
docs.text:
The interactive graph is powered by vis.js and provides:

  • DAG-based hierarchical layout
  • Color-coded nodes by system (Confluent, Databricks, AWS, External)
  • Click-to-inspect detail panels
  • Multi-select with Shift+drag
  • Search by qualified name
  • Export as JSON

See the Graph Visualization Guide for interaction details.

docs.title: Change Detection
docs.text:
The watcher monitors Confluent Cloud for lineage-relevant changes and triggers automatic re-extraction:

  • REST API polling mode (default)
  • Audit log Kafka consumer mode (optional)
  • Configurable cooldown period
  • Event history and extraction logs

See the Change Detection Guide for setup and usage.

docs.title: 1. Extract Lineage (CLI)
docs.text:
# Extract from one environment
lineage-bridge-extract --env env-abc123

# Extract from multiple environments with filters
lineage-bridge-extract \
  --env env-abc123 \
  --env env-def456 \
  --cluster lkc-xyz789 \
  --output my-graph.json

# Enrich an existing graph
lineage-bridge-extract --enrich-only --output my-graph.json

docs.title: 2. Start the UI
docs.text:
# Launch Streamlit UI
lineage-bridge-ui
# or
uv run streamlit run lineage_bridge/ui/app.py

Open http://localhost:8501, connect, select infrastructure, and extract.

docs.title: 3. Watch for Changes
docs.text:
# Monitor and auto-extract
lineage-bridge-watch --env env-abc123 --cooldown 30

# Push to Databricks after each extraction
lineage-bridge-watch --env env-abc123 --push-uc

docs.title: 4. Start the API
